exemplar.title: To what extent have Nikeâs unethical labor practices affected its reputation and sales?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have Nikeâs unethical labor practices affected its reputation and sales?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear identification and discussion of ethics as the key concept throughout major sections
Effective linkage of ethical issues to organisational outcomes such as profit margin and reputation
Use of specific Nike data (revenues, margins, shareholder motions) to illustrate ethical considerations
weaknesses: Lacks integration of formal ethical theories (e.g., utilitarianism, deontology) for deeper analytical rigor
Ethics lens is not woven into every paragraph, with some descriptive passages remaining unlinked
Occasional assertions (e.g., margin changes due to wage cuts) are not fully supported by Nike-specific evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student demonstrates a good understanding of ethics and integrates it clearly in key sections, the concept is not sustained throughout every analytical paragraph and lacks theoretical depth. This aligns best with band 3 (good understanding with clear analytical thinking) rather than band 4.

strengths: All six sources directly relate to Nikeâs labour practices or financial performance
Use of diverse media sources (news articles, campaign websites, Statista) shows breadth of research
weaknesses: Number of documents exceeds the 3â5 range, placing it in the lowest band
Sources lack academic or primary-data depth, relying heavily on secondary news articles and snapshots
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the sources are relevant, providing more than five documents automatically places this work in band 1 for the number criterion. Depth is superficial, so the overall score for Supporting Documents is 1.

strengths: Appropriate use of stakeholder mapping and quantitative tools (trend graph, margin table)
Applied each tool directly to the research question on Nikeâs ethics and outcomes
weaknesses: Selection of tools is limited and lacks sophistication (no CSR pyramid, elasticity, sentiment indexes)
Application sometimes makes assumptive links without sufficient evidence (e.g., profits linked to wage under-payment)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student selects some appropriate tools, but application is superficial and relevance is not always clearly evidenced. This corresponds to band 2 (some tools selected with superficial relevance).
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective selection and use of revenue time-series and financial data to support argument
Mostly effective analysis linking ethical pressures to sales and profit outcomes
Engages with causal mechanisms and counterarguments in several sections
weaknesses: Data scope is limited (no regional breakdowns, reputation metrics)
Some evaluation lacks depth (e.g., cost breakdown evidence, segmentation data)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis goes beyond description, offering sustained integration of ethical issues with financial outcomes and evaluating causal factors. This merits band 4 for mostly effective analysis with some integration.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the presented evidence
Explicitly and directly answer the research question regarding reputation and sales impact
Forward-looking recommendations align with the analysis
weaknesses: Could further synthesize findings into concise bullet points for clarity
Minor repetition in phrasing reduces impact
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides conclusions that are fully consistent with evidence and explicitly answer the research question, matching band 3.

strengths: Clear structure with contents page, logical headings, and coherent flow
Adheres to IB conventions and supports navigation effectively
weaknesses: Some minor repetitions and misplaced details
Transition between certain sections could be smoother with additional linking sentences
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IAâs organisation is appropriate throughout with clear structure, meriting the top band (band 2).

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements: title page, word count, RQ, in-text citations, works-cited list
Consistent formatting and adherence to IB presentation standards
weaknesses: Citation style consistency could be improved (access dates, style uniformity)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required elements are present and properly formatted, placing this submission in band 2 for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows IB conventions, provides a contents page and logical headings, and ideas flow coherently.  Minor repetition and some misplaced details exist, but overall organisation is appropriate.

All sources are directly related to Nikeâs labour practices or its financial performance, but they are predominantly news articles, campaign websites and Statista snapshots.  Depth is limitedâno primary data or academic studiesâso relevance is sound while depth remains superficial.
The submission includes all required elements: title page with RQ and word count, contents, labelled sections, in-text citations and a works-cited list, satisfying the top band for presentation.
Six distinct secondary sources are cited, exceeding the 3-5 range stipulated; according to the rubric, providing more than five places the work in the â1-2 or more than 5 documentsâ band.

Each tool is applied to Nike, and conclusions are drawn, yet links are sometimes assumptive (e.g. equating margin rise with wage under-payment).  Overall relevance is clear but not consistently evidenced, matching âadequately applied; relevance not always clearâ.

Revenue time-series data and 2023-24 financial figures are correctly selected and used to support discussion.  Data scope is limited (no reputation metrics, no regional splits), but the material chosen is appropriate and utilised with basic numerical commentary.

The narrative goes beyond description by weighing stakeholder pressure against sales growth and discussing possible causal mechanisms.  Integration between ethical issues and financial outcomes is mostly effective, though depth and counter-arguments are thin in places, so the work fits âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ.

The student explicitly answers the research question, stating the extent of impact on both reputation and sales and offering forward-looking recommendations, meeting the descriptor for a clear, explicit answer.

The student employs stakeholder mapping and two simple quantitative tools (trend graph, margin table).  This constitutes an adequate, although not extensive, toolkit for the RQ; more sophisticated models (e.g. CSR pyramid, elasticity or sentiment indices) are absent.

The concluding section summarises findings that align with earlier evidence (e.g., sales continued to rise; reputation risk remains).  No claims contradict presented data, satisfying full consistency.

Nike is consistently referenced, with specific data (revenues 2016-24, profit-margin table, mention of Nike Direct, shareholder motions) forming the basis of argument.  The analysis links the ethical issue to concrete organisational outcomes, yet some parts (e.g. speculation that higher margins may stem from wage cuts) are asserted without supporting Nike-specific evidence, so the integration stops short of being fully comprehensive.

The student keeps the lens of ethics visible in each main section and links it to stakeholder, revenue and margin discussions, showing clear awareness of why labour practices matter.  However, the concept is not woven into every paragraph of the financial analysis, and there is little theoretical discussion of ethical frameworks beyond description, so the integration is only partial rather than sustained throughout.

annotations.comment: The research question is precise and focused, providing a clear scope for analysis on reputational and sales impacts.
Identifying ethics as the core concept is appropriate, but the discussion would benefit from integrating ethical theories (e.g., utilitarianism, deontology) to deepen analysis.
Title page includes all required elements (RQ, concept, word count), demonstrating adherence to IB presentation standards.
Table of contents is comprehensive with page references, supporting navigation through the IA effectively.
Consider including a brief methodology overview in the contents to signal the analytical tools used and strengthen structure.
Early analysis sentence is descriptive; strengthen by linking forced labor allegations to brand equity models.
Descriptions of layoffs and pay cuts are well-sourced but lack specific figures on number of employees affected.
Use of NGO and campaign sources is relevant, but incorporate academic or primary data to deepen document depth.
Stakeholder analysis description is thorough; adding a visual map (power-interest grid) would clarify stakeholder positions.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Employee stakeholder analysis connects ethics to morale and productivity; integrate ethical framework terminology for rigor.
Attributing margin changes to unethical practices requires cost breakdown evidence; source cost-of-goods data for validation.
Section numbering is clear, but the jump from âAnalysisâ to â1. Stakeholder Analysisâ could be prefaced by a methodology heading.
The introduction provides solid company background but should contextualize the $51.2 bn revenue by comparing to industry benchmarks.
The methodological overview clearly outlines stakeholder, graph, and margin analyses, aligning tools with the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Shareholder section links profit concerns to ethics; quantify potential share price or dividend impacts to support evaluation.
Customer analysis notes eco-conscious demand appropriately but lacks segmentation data (e.g., % of sales from ethical consumers).
Citing Ethical Consumer is apt; avoid generalizing boycott impact without linking to actual sales figures.
Government stakeholder discussion on UFLPA is strong; include Nikeâs current compliance measures to balance perspective.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The stakeholder analysis summary effectively links stakeholders to outcomes; discuss model limitations (e.g., dynamic influence changes).
Graph is well placed but lacks axis labels and units; include these to improve clarity and interpretation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Revenue trend interpretation touches on growth but would benefit from a simple regression or significance test.
Introduce a brief rationale for choosing graph analysis as a tool to strengthen methodological transparency.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Analysis of alternative growth factors acknowledges complexity; quantify price impact versus ethical reputation loss for balance.
Recommend including a sensitivity or scenario analysis to test the link between labor cost changes and profit margin shifts.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Transition into the profit margin analysis is logical, but add a linking sentence to reinforce flow from sales to margin.
Table presents 2023â24 data clearly; consider adding earlier years for trend context and deeper evaluation.
Linking profit margin resilience back to ethics demonstrates solid concept integration across financial analysis.
Avoid redundant phrasing in conclusion; streamline statements to reinforce key insights without repetition.
Works Cited entries follow a consistent format but verify adherence to the chosen citation style (MLA/APA).
Bibliography shows breadth of media sources but lacks peer-reviewed or academic journal references for depth.
Ensure all online sources include an access date to maintain academic rigour in citations.
Conclusion section is clearly marked; summarizing key findings in bullets could improve readability.
Concluding recommendations align with evidence and directly answer the research question, demonstrating synthesis.
Limitations section is insightful; expand on methods for gathering primary data (e.g., surveys, interviews) to guide future research.
